propositions: The only publication gate checks the ref is a v* tag pushed, without verifying the tagged SHA is an ancestor of reviewed main.
Version values across pyproject.toml, server.json, and server.json.packages are kept in lockstep only by a manual runbook, risking mismatched versions published across PyPI/GitHub/MCP channels.
Releases should be initiated via workflow_dispatch from the protected main branch so the released source is reviewed by construction.
A version-check job must assert version_input equals all manifest versions and fail before build and before tag creation on any mismatch.
An ancestry assertion must confirm the release SHA is reachable from origin/main before the tag is created and on the retained raw tag-push fallback.
Production publication must require an independent human approval via the required-reviewers pypi environment, without restricting who can push tags.
